title: cyclobalanus costata
intro: Cyclobalanus costata, also known as the Pao-Shan-Hua or the Golden Camellia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Theaceae, native to the mountainous regions of southern China and northern Vietnam. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Cyclobalanus costata include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as promoting wound healing and immune system function. Cyclobalanus costata can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and health benefits.
